turns.student: What brings you in today?
turns.patient: I've been having some really bad abdominal pain and bloody stools for about 3 weeks now. The pain is crampy and mostly on my left side, and it feels like I have to go to the bathroom all the time - I'm talking 8-10 times a day, with loose watery stool that's mixed with bright red blood. It's been really tough to manage and it's affecting my work performance because of the fatigue.

turns.student: Can you describe the main symptom you're experiencing?
turns.patient: The crampy abdominal pain is the worst part, it's constant and feels like a knife stabbing on my left side. And then there's the bloody stools - it's just really scary to see blood in my stool. The diarrhea is also super uncomfortable, it's loose and watery and I feel like I'm always on edge wondering when I'll have another episode.

turns.student: When did this start?
turns.patient: It started about 3 weeks ago, but to be honest, I've been having some digestive issues for a while now. Like, I'd get the occasional stomach bug or something, but nothing like this. It's like my body just flipped a switch and I went from being fine to this non-stop diarrhea with blood in it.

turns.student: How often does it happen?
turns.patient: It happens pretty much every time I go to the bathroom now, which is 8-10 times a day. Sometimes it's worse than others - there have been days where I've had to change my clothes multiple times because of accidents.

turns.student: How severe is it on a scale of 1 to 10?
turns.patient: It's a solid 9 out of 10, honestly. The pain and the frequency of the diarrhea are really affecting my daily life and causing me a lot of discomfort. I'd say the only thing that keeps it from being a 10 is that it doesn't happen at night – most times, I can get some rest without having to worry about an episode.

turns.student: Do you notice any other symptoms when it happens?
turns.patient: I've been feeling really fatigued lately, even after getting plenty of sleep. It's like my body is constantly drained and I don't have the energy to do much of anything, which is making work really tough for me. And sometimes, especially when I'm having a bad episode, I feel like I'm going to pass out or get dizzy - it's pretty scary, actually.

turns.student: Does it get worse with any particular activity?
turns.patient: Actually, it seems to be related to my stress levels. When I'm working long hours and feeling really overwhelmed, the symptoms tend to worsen. I've noticed that if I take a break or try to manage my stress through exercise or meditation, the pain and diarrhea seem to subside for a bit.

turns.student: Do you have any sweating, nausea, or dizziness with it?
turns.patient: The fatigue is definitely affecting my work performance. It's not just physical, but also mental - I feel like I'm constantly exhausted and can't think clearly, even when I try to push through. As for your question about sweating, nausea, or dizziness, I do get a bit lightheaded during really bad episodes, especially if I've been standing up for a while beforehand. But as for sweating or feeling nauseous, that's not usually part of it for me.

turns.student: Does the pain or discomfort spread to any other part of your body?
turns.patient: To be honest, sometimes my joints feel achy, especially in my knees and ankles. It's not a sharp pain like I'm having with my abdomen, but more of a dull ache that feels like it's always there. It doesn't seem to be related to the abdominal symptoms, though - it's just this nagging feeling that I've had for a while now.

turns.student: Do you have any swelling in your legs or feet?
turns.patient: No, I don't have any noticeable swelling in my legs or feet. But to be honest, the joint pain and fatigue are making it hard for me to do much of anything, let alone stay active enough to notice if I'm getting swollen or not.

turns.student: What medications are you currently taking?
turns.patient: I've been self-medicating with loperamide (Imodium) for diarrhea for the past 2 weeks, but it's only given me minimal relief. Other than that, I don't take any regular medication.

turns.student: Do you have any medical conditions like high blood pressure or diabetes?
turns.patient: I've been taking ibuprofen a few times a week for my joint pain in my knees and ankles, but it seems to make the abdominal symptoms worse. As for other medical conditions, I don't have any known issues with high blood pressure or diabetes. My father has type 2 diabetes, though.

turns.student: Have you ever had your cholesterol checked?
turns.patient: I haven't had my cholesterol checked recently. To be honest, I've been putting off seeing a doctor for a while now because of work and other stuff, so it's probably been over two years since I last saw one about anything health-related.

turns.student: Do you smoke or drink alcohol?
turns.patient: I'm a non-smoker, but I do drink 2-3 beers most weeknights to help manage my stress. Lately, though, I've increased it to 4-5 beers per night because of how overwhelmed I feel with work and these symptoms. It's not something I'd normally do, but it feels like a coping mechanism right now.

turns.student: How would you describe your diet and exercise habits?
turns.patient:
To be honest, my diet has been pretty irregular lately due to long work hours and stress. I usually end up eating takeout or grabbing something quick on the go, which isn't great for me. As for exercise, I used to try to stay active by going for runs or lifting weights, but with these symptoms, it's been tough to keep that up. I've been trying to get in some yoga or meditation to help manage my stress and alleviate some of the joint pain, but it's not as consistent as I'd like it to be.

I've also noticed that when I do eat, it tends to trigger more diarrhea episodes, especially if I consume anything spicy or fatty. It's like my stomach is super sensitive right now, and even normal foods can set me off.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had heart disease or heart attacks?
turns.patient: No, no one in my immediate family has had any issues with heart disease or heart attacks that I'm aware of. My father has type 2 diabetes, but like I mentioned earlier, he's been managing it pretty well.

turns.student: Has anyone in your family had other serious medical conditions?
turns.patient: My older sister was diagnosed with Crohn's disease at age 30, so there is a family history of inflammatory bowel disease on my mom's side.

turns.student: When was the last time you saw a doctor?
turns.patient: It's been over two years since I last saw a doctor about anything health-related. Like I mentioned earlier, I've been putting off seeing one because of work and other stuff. To be honest, I've been feeling pretty overwhelmed with these symptoms and haven't known where to start or who to turn to for help.

turns.student: Do you have any allergies to medications?
turns.patient: No, I don't have any known drug allergies. I'm actually concerned about potential interactions between my stress medication and the ibuprofen I've been taking for joint pain, though - it seems like the ibuprofen makes my abdominal symptoms worse.

turns.student: Is there anything else you think I should know about your health?
turns.patient: I just want to add that I've tried to manage my stress levels on my own by exercising and meditating, but it's been really tough lately. The symptoms seem to be getting worse, not better, despite my best efforts. And to be honest, I'm worried about what might be causing this - is it something serious like an infection or a more chronic condition? That uncertainty is making me really anxious.
